# `combine` Function

An opinionated setproduct for objects

Combine a map of lists or maps into a single map with an item for each combination of items in the lists or maps

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
combine(input dynamic, separator string...) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) Ordered list of {key, items} objects to combine. Each object has a 'key' string (the attribute name in the result) and an 'items' list or map. When 'items' is a map of objects, each result entry's dimension attribute is set to the map value merged with an 'id' field containing the map key.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `separator` (Variadic, String) Separator string used to generate the keys in the combined map. Defaults to '/'.

## Example Usage

### Basic usage

Each dimension has a `key` (the attribute name in the result) and an `items` list. The function produces one entry per combination, with the map key built by joining dimension values with the separator.

```terraform
locals {
  dimensions = [
    { key = "env",    items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region", items = ["europe-west1", "europe-north1"] },
  ]
}

output "combinations" {
  value = provider::util::combine(local.dimensions)
}
# Result:
# {
#   "dev/europe-west1"   = { env = "dev",  region = "europe-west1"  }
#   "dev/europe-north1"  = { env = "dev",  region = "europe-north1" }
#   "prod/europe-west1"  = { env = "prod", region = "europe-west1"  }
#   "prod/europe-north1" = { env = "prod", region = "europe-north1" }
# }
```

### Custom separator

Pass a separator string to control how the map keys are joined.

```terraform
locals {
  dimensions = [
    { key = "env",     items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "service", items = ["api", "web"] },
  ]
}

output "underscore_keys" {
  value = provider::util::combine(local.dimensions, "_")
}
# Result keys: "dev_api", "dev_web", "prod_api", "prod_web"
```

### Using map items

Instead of a list, each dimension can use a map for `items`. The map key is used as the path segment. When map values are objects, the result attribute for that dimension is set to the map value's attributes **plus** an `id` field holding the map key.

```terraform
locals {
  dimensions = [
    { key = "env", items = {
      dev  = { debug = true,  replicas = 1 }
      prod = { debug = false, replicas = 3 }
    }},
    { key = "tier", items = ["api", "web"] },
  ]
}

output "with_metadata" {
  value = provider::util::combine(local.dimensions)
}
# Result:
# {
#   "dev/api"  = { env = { id = "dev",  debug = true,  replicas = 1 }, tier = "api" }
#   "dev/web"  = { env = { id = "dev",  debug = true,  replicas = 1 }, tier = "web" }
#   "prod/api" = { env = { id = "prod", debug = false, replicas = 3 }, tier = "api" }
#   "prod/web" = { env = { id = "prod", debug = false, replicas = 3 }, tier = "web" }
# }
```

### Using combine with for_each

The flat map output is designed for use with `for_each`, allowing you to express multi-dimensional resources concisely.

```terraform
locals {
  combos = provider::util::combine([
    { key = "env",    items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region", items = ["europe-west1", "us-central1"] },
  ])
}

resource "google_compute_network" "vpc" {
  for_each = local.combos
  name     = "vpc-${each.value.env}-${each.value.region}"
}
```
